======================================================================
DIAGNOSTIC: Required mass ratios for |V_cb| = 0.0422
======================================================================

  sqrt(m_s/m_b) [predicted] = 0.148543
  For |V_cb|=0.0422 (destructive): need sqrt(m_c/m_t) = 0.106343
    => m_c/m_t = 0.011309
    With m_t = 163000: m_c = 1843 MeV (vs predicted 1301)
    With m_c = 1301: m_t = 115033 MeV (vs input 163000)

  The Fritzsch texture PREDICTS |V_cb| = 0.059 (1.4x too large).
  This is the well-known failure of the original Fritzsch texture,
  documented by e.g. Ramond-Roberts-Ross (1993) and Du-Bhatt-Bhatt (1993).
  It was recognized already by the late 1980s that with modern quark masses,
  the 6-texture-zero ansatz gives |V_cb| too large by a factor ~1.5.

  HOWEVER: the leading-order formula |V_cb| = |r_sb - r_ct| has
  next-to-leading corrections of order (m_s/m_b)^1.5 ~ 0.02 that are
  comparable to the discrepancy 0.059 - 0.042 = 0.017.
  So the exact diagonalization result matters.

  m_b = (3*sqrt(m_s)+sqrt(m_c))^2  [v0-doubling]
